channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Northeast Monsoon Expected to Weaken Soon, Amihan Season to End
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) announced that the northeast monsoon, locally known as amihan, is weakening and may end either tomorrow or by ...

item.title: MCD tells Delhi High Court it will not demolish Uttam Nagar homes without notice in Holi clash case
item.description: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) has told the Delhi High Court that it will not carry out any demolition of homes in Uttam Nagar linked to the Holi‑day clash case without issuing a prior writt...

item.title: How Famous Nigerian Weddings Take Over Social Media From 2017 to 2026
item.description: For Naija, celebrity weddings no be small thing — dem dey always turn internet upside down. From the time of Banky W and Adesua Etomi for 2017 reach Simi and Femi Dapson for 2026, these love events do...

item.title: Nigel Farage criticised after Cameo videos surface with far-right references
item.description: A series of videos recorded by Nigel Farage on Cameo have come under scrutiny after they were found to contain remarks and slogans associated with far-right and neo-Nazi movements. The clips, uncovere...

item.title: Trump Confirms Willingness to Deploy US Ground Troops in Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump declared on 17 March 2026 that he is 'not afraid' to deploy US ground troops in Iran, even in a conflict reminiscent of the Vietnam War. Speaking from the Oval Office alongside ...

item.title: CFMEU Inquiry Examines Influence on Cross River Rail Project Management
item.description: The CFMEU inquiry, launched by the Crisafulli government at a cost of $19.7 million, continues to examine allegations of criminality, corruption, and misconduct within unions and the construction sect...

item.title: Kanlaon Volcano Eruption Disrupts Classes for Over 126,000 Students
item.description: The moderately explosive eruption of Kanlaon Volcano on Negros Island has disrupted classes for 126,572 students, according to the Department of Education (DepEd). The eruption occurred on March 16, 2...

item.title: Morogoro Road Upgrade: Diversions in Place to Ease Traffic During Construction
item.description: Motorists using Morogoro Road, especially the busy Kimara–Ubungo section, are being advised to follow diversion routes while workers remove old asphalt and lay a new, stronger road surface. The faceli...

item.title: Atiku's Sons' Rival Ambitions Cause Division in Adamawa ADC Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Big wahala don dey inside African Democratic Congress (ADC) for Adamawa State as two sons of former Vice President Atiku Abubakar, Adamu and Mohammed, dey pursue the same House of Representatives seat...

item.title: Northern Senators Condemn Maiduguri Bomb Attacks, Promise Federal Assistance
item.description: The Northern Senators Forum don express deep shock and sorrow after the deadly bomb explosions wey happen for Maiduguri, Borno State, on Monday evening. According to the statement wey dem release on T...

item.title: Arsenal Gained Advantage From Three VAR Decisions This Premier League Season
item.description: An independent audit has revealed that Arsenal benefitted from three notable VAR errors in the ongoing Premier League season. These incidents, all involving penalty decisions, occurred in matches that...

item.title: Gilas Women secure first-ever win over a non-Asian opponent in FIBA World Cup Qualifiers
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Gilas Women made history by defeating world No. 19 Colombia, 74-59, in the FIBA Women’s World Cup Qualifying Tournament held in Lyon-Villeurbanne, France, on March 18, 2026. This...

item.title: Australian and US Markets Rise Amid Middle East Tensions and Rising Oil Prices
item.description: Australian shares are expected to open slightly higher, reflecting a generally positive sentiment from Wall Street. Oil prices continued to climb due to the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran. Isra...

item.title: Federal, KP, and Sindh governments declare Eidul Fitr holidays from March 19 to 21
item.description: The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa government declared a three-day public holiday for Eidul Fitr 2026, from March 19 to 21 (Friday to Sunday). According to the official notification, the holidays will be observed...

item.title: Kerri Einarson Guides Canada to Fifth Consecutive Win at Women’s World Curling Championship
item.description: Canada’s skip Kerri Einarson delivered a strong performance at the women’s world curling championship in Calgary, securing her team’s fifth straight victory with a 9-6 win over Italy’s Stefania Consta...

item.title: Simulations Reveal How Craters Could Reveal the Interior of Asteroid Psyche
item.description: Asteroid 16 Psyche, a metal-rich object located in the main asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter, has puzzled scientists for over two centuries. At roughly 140 miles in diameter, Psyche is the large...

item.title: Zanzibar Government to Build Major International Conference Centre at Fumba
item.description: The Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar (RGoZ) has announced plans to construct the Fumba International Conference and Trade Exhibition Centre, costing over $124.5 million (around Sh324.68 billion). ...

item.title: Tinubu Directs Political Appointees with 2027 Ambitions to Step Down by March 31
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don issue order say all political appointees wey dey under him government and wey wan contest for 2027 general elections suppose resign their posts latest by March 31, 2026. The ...

item.title: Trainer John O’Shea appeals four-month ban for misconduct towards Racing NSW veterinarians
item.description: Multiple Group 1-winning trainer John O’Shea has lodged an appeal against the severity of a four-month disqualification handed down for improper conduct towards two Racing NSW veterinarians. The charg...

item.title: Court Orders Olabisi Onabanjo University Hospital to Reinstate Nurse and Pay Back Pay
item.description: The National Industrial Court of Nigeria for Lagos don give judgement wey favour Mrs. Ajibola Aishat Oluwafunke, nurse wey dem sack from Olabisi Onabanjo University Teaching Hospital in November 2020....
